question: Write a C++ program that merges two arrays A and B into array C using a specific pattern. Array C should contain elements in the order: even-indexed elements from B (in reverse), odd-indexed elements from A (in reverse), then remaining elements from both arrays. Display all arrays.

test_cases.expected:
Enter size of array A (M): Enter size of array B (N): Enter elements of array A: Enter elements of array B: 
Array A: 3 2 1 7 6 3 
Array B: 9 3 5 6 2 8 10 
Array C: 2 6 6 2 8 10 5 3 9 3 7 1 3

errors.description: No input validation: Program doesn't check for negative or zero array sizes, which could cause undefined behavior.
No bounds checking: Variable-length arrays with unvalidated sizes could cause stack overflow or memory issues.
Array bounds violation: leftIndex could exceed array bounds when copying elements without proper validation.
Potential uninitialized memory access: Array C might contain uninitialized values if leftIndex and rightIndex don't cover all positions.
